admissibility: trade_secret — NDA required for delta/FDR values

programs.what_zeb1_is: ZEB1 (Zinc finger E-box binding homeobox 1) is a master regulator of epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT). In NSCLC/BrM, ZEB1-high tumors have undergone EMT — they are more invasive, more metastatic, and more resistant to chemotherapy. ZEB1-high is a marker of aggressive disease.
programs.what_itgav_is: ITGAV (Integrin alpha-V) is a cell surface receptor that mediates cell adhesion to the extracellular matrix. In ZEB1-high cells, ITGAV becomes essential for survival — ZEB1-high cells depend on integrin signaling for anoikis resistance and survival in the metastatic niche.
programs.why_sl_makes_sense: ZEB1-high cells have undergone EMT and lost epithelial adhesion molecules (E-cadherin). They become dependent on mesenchymal adhesion molecules (integrins, including ITGAV) for survival. Inhibiting ITGAV in ZEB1-high cells removes their survival scaffold — they die. ZEB1-low cells retain E-cadherin and are not dependent on ITGAV.
programs.licensing_value: Benchmark: Roche paid $125M upfront for Repare's camonsertib (ATRi) — one CRISPR-validated SL asset. BRIEF-1 is a CRISPR-validated SL asset in a different indication (NSCLC/BrM). Licensing target: $8–15M upfront (Months 6–12 per $500M roadmap).
programs.admissibility_note: Delta and FDR values are trade secrets — do not publish in external outputs without NDA

programs.what_spp1_is: SPP1 (Secreted Phosphoprotein 1, also known as Osteopontin) is a secreted glycoprotein that promotes tumor invasion, metastasis, and immune evasion. SPP1-high tumors have an immunosuppressive TME — SPP1 recruits MDSCs and promotes M2 macrophage polarization.
programs.what_nrf2_is: NRF2 (Nuclear factor erythroid 2-related factor 2) is a transcription factor that regulates the antioxidant response. In SPP1-high cells, NRF2 becomes essential for managing oxidative stress — SPP1-high cells generate more reactive oxygen species (ROS) and depend on NRF2 for survival.
programs.why_sl_makes_sense: SPP1-high cells have elevated oxidative stress from their pro-inflammatory, pro-metastatic activity. They become dependent on NRF2 for ROS management. Inhibiting NRF2 in SPP1-high cells overwhelms their antioxidant capacity — they die from oxidative stress. SPP1-low cells have lower ROS and are not dependent on NRF2.
programs.status_note: COMPUTATIONAL_LEAD — CRISPR-validated but not yet deal-ready. Requires additional validation before licensing discussion.

programs.rationale: ZEB1-high tumors upregulate PD-L1 expression (ZEB1 directly transcribes PD-L1). ZEB1-high + PD-1 blockade is mechanistically grounded — ZEB1-high tumors should be more responsive to checkpoint blockade.
programs.correlation_result: GSE271259 (n=34): r=0.050, p=0.780 — statistically INSIGNIFICANT. The correlation between ZEB1 expression and PD-1 response is not supported by this dataset.
programs.mandatory_disclosure: BRIEF-3 correlation r=0.050, p=0.780 is statistically insignificant. BRIEF-3 is MECHANISTICALLY_GROUNDED, not CRISPR_VALIDATED. The combination hypothesis is supported by mechanistic literature but not by the GSE271259 correlation.

programs.rationale: PTEN-null cells lose PI3K/AKT regulation and may become dependent on integrin signaling (ITGAV) for survival — similar to ZEB1-high cells.
programs.depmap_result: DepMap NULL across all 3 proxies — ITGAV is NOT more essential in PTEN-null cells than PTEN-intact cells in the DepMap data.
programs.mandatory_disclosure: BRIEF-2 (PTEN-null→ITGAV) is a COMPUTATIONAL HYPOTHESIS. DepMap shows NULL essentiality across all 3 proxies. Not CRISPR-validated. Requires experimental validation before any licensing discussion. Must be disclosed in every context where BRIEF-2 appears.

ip_valuation.thesis: Pharma spent $1.95 billion running six brain cancer trials that failed for reasons a CRISPR functional genomics engine could have identified before a single patient was enrolled. CrisPRO decoded all six failures, identified the correct patient populations, and filed four patent claims covering the corrected indications.

brief_1_licensing_value.benchmark: Roche paid $125M upfront for Repare's camonsertib (ATRi) — one CRISPR-validated SL asset in a different indication
brief_1_licensing_value.note: All deal figures are benchmarks from comparable transactions — not projections or guarantees

ip_valuation.repare_benchmark: ~$400M Series B on ONE CRISPR-validated SL asset

governance.pten_null_status: COMPUTATIONAL HYPOTHESIS — DepMap NULL across all 3 proxies — not CRISPR-validated
governance.deal_structures: All deal figures are benchmarks from comparable transactions — not guaranteed outcomes

patent.title: System and Method for Predictive Patient-to-Intervention Mechanism Alignment for Clinical Trial Stratification, Arm Selection, and Cohort Enrichment Using Shared Multi-Dimensional Biological Vector Spaces

what_is_patented.core_invention: A computer-implemented method for encoding a patient state and a therapeutic intervention state in a shared biologically defined mechanism space, computing alignment between the encoded states, and using the resulting outputs for trial ranking, arm selection, eligibility-gated stratification, cohort enrichment, and adaptive trial operations.
key_claims: Claim 1: Computer-implemented method for improving clinical trial enrollment efficiency using multi-dimensional biological mechanism space vectors
Claim 2: The method of Claim 1, wherein the mechanism space dimensions correspond to DDR, MAPK, PI3K, VEGF, HER2, IO, efflux, and RSS pathways
Claim 3: The method of Claim 1, wherein alignment is computed as clip((p·t)/‖t‖₂, 0, 1) — PATH A formula
Claim 4: The method of Claim 1, applied to synthetic lethality asset identification (BRIEF programs)
what_prior_art_cannot_do: ClinicalTrials.gov keyword matching: Boolean match only — no quantitative alignment score
OncoKB/CIVIC: Map mutations to drugs but no patient-trial alignment scoring
Standard biomarker matching: Single-gene matching — no multi-dimensional mechanism space

patent.ip_moat: The patent covers the multi-dimensional mechanism space approach — not just the formula. Any system that encodes patient biology and trial mechanism in a shared vector space and computes alignment falls within the claims. This is broader than the specific PATH A formula.
